root_text.sn45.76:1.2: Yonisomanasikārasampannassetaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno pāṭikaṅkhaṁ—ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāvessati, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkarissati.
root_text.sn45.76:1.3: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu yonisomanasikārasampanno ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāveti, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkaroti?
root_text.sn45.76:1.4: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sammādiṭṭhiṁ bhāveti …pe… sammāsamādhiṁ bhāveti rāgavinayapariyosānaṁ dosavinayapariyosānaṁ mohavinayapariyosānaṁ.
root_text.sn45.76:1.5: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu yonisomanasikārasampanno ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāveti, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkarotī”ti.

translation_text.sn45.76:0.3: Accomplishment in Rational Application of Mind (2nd)
translation_text.sn45.76:1.1: “… accomplishment in rational application of mind.
translation_text.sn45.76:1.2: A mendicant accomplished in rational application of mind can expect to develop and cultivate the noble eightfold path.
translation_text.sn45.76:1.3: And how does a mendicant accomplished in rational application of mind develop and cultivate the noble eightfold path?
translation_text.sn45.76:1.4: It’s when a mendicant develops right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion, which culminate in the removal of greed, hate, and delusion.
translation_text.sn45.76:1.5: That’s how a mendicant accomplished in rational application of mind develops and cultivates the noble eightfold path.”
